per_turn_leaks.student_message: Can you describe the main symptom you're experiencing?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: The crampy lower abdominal pain is pretty constant, but it's always on my left side. The worst part is when I have a bowel movement, because that's when the bloody stools usually occur, and it's just really scary to see blood in my stool.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Do you notice any other symptoms when it happens?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: To be honest, yes... I do get a little lightheaded and sweaty when the pain is really bad. It's like my whole body just goes into shock or something. And sometimes, after I've had a bowel movement, I feel like I haven't completely emptied out - it's hard to explain, but it's like there's still stuff in there that needs to come out.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Does the pain or discomfort spread to any other part of your body?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Actually, yeah... my knees and ankles have been hurting a lot lately too. It's just a dull ache, nothing that's really swollen or red, but it's definitely there all the time. I've just assumed it was from working long hours on my feet, but now that you mention it, maybe it's related to what's going on in my gut?

per_turn_leaks.student_message: What medications are you currently taking?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I'm actually taking loperamide, which is Imodium, for the diarrhea. I started taking it about two weeks ago in an attempt to control the symptoms, but it's only given me minimal relief at best. As for other medications, I've been taking ibuprofen a few times a week for my joint pain, but like I said, that seems to make things worse when I'm already experiencing abdominal symptoms.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Do you smoke or drink alcohol?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I don't smoke at all. As for drinking, I do have an occasional beer on weeknights, but I've actually increased my consumption to 4-5 beers per night over the past month because of stress from work and these symptoms. It's not something I'm proud of, but it's just been a way to try and cope with everything that's going on.
